highlights.text: I stated at Monday’s close that: “Its been a hugely profitable run and I am +250 points now on my most recent long..My general lean is always to defer to the trend until it ends.
They put in my core setup, the Failed Breakdown, whereby they flush a major low, trap shorts, recover the low, then squeeze.

As readers know, last Wednesday after FOMC, we saw a massive Failed Breakdown where we flushed the prior days low at 5606, recovered, and I was long since then for what turned into a 300+ point rally, one of the largest of the year.
This Failed Breakdown was also the final trigger as well for the breakout of a 1 week bull flag pattern that built out mostly between 5585 and 5650, into last Wednesday.
This means bulls want to hold 5850, 5815 on any CPI flushes (I’ll be watching for failed breakdowns).

Breakout targets 5945, 5970, 5983, then 5600” Overnight we got this, we dipped under the flag, recovered it by this morning to put in my core setup: The Failed Breakdown, and ran to 5945 1st target which was the high of day.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into two quality Failed Breakdown we had on Tuesday which produced Tuesdays rally from sub 5850s to 5927 and yesterday.
Typically, we get one Failed Breakdown per day, and these two were a nice contrast between what I call a “complex” Failed Breakdown, and a “simple” Failed Breakdown.
Next target slate is 5890, 5910, 5926.” This played out perfectly.

post_title: 4 Green Days In A Row. Can SPX Close Week With 5, Or Are Bulls Out Of Steam? May 16 Plan
